codeReview.summary: EditorView, MarkdownEditor, and MarkdownPreview already satisfy the functional workspace requirements (dual panels, textarea sync, placeholder/spellcheck, and markdown rendering), but the feature still misses its explicit styling contract: the workspace panel surfaces are not fully tokenized.

issues.description: The Script panel surface still hard-codes its core background and shadow colors (`rgba(...)`, `#0a0a0c`) instead of using design-token variables. Because the feature's expectedBehavior explicitly requires both panels to use design tokens for styling, this leaves the redesign only partially implemented.

The Preview panel surface also keeps hard-coded highlight/base/shadow colors (`rgba(...)`, `#101013`) rather than token-backed values. This means the second workspace panel still fails the same design-token requirement from the feature contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library does not contain a design-token reference even though workspace-redesign workers try to consult one, so they end up rediscovering token details by reading `src/index.css` directly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The reviewed transcript skeleton for workerSessionId `430ababd-13bf-4903-84da-ffbc60bcd084` begins with `Read D:\coding\somewhat\.factory\library\design-tokens.md`, but `D:\coding\somewhat\.factory\library` currently only contains `architecture.md`, `environment.md`, and `user-testing.md`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `frontend-redesign` skill says workers must invoke `frontend-design` at the start, but this transcript shows `mission-worker-base` and later `frontend-redesign` only. Either that requirement is not actually needed in practice, or the skill/handoff guidance is out of sync.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `D:\coding\somewhat\.factory\skills\frontend-redesign\SKILL.md:19-23` requires `frontend-design` at the START. The transcript skeleton for workerSessionId `430ababd-13bf-4903-84da-ffbc60bcd084` shows `Skill {"skill":"mission-worker-base"}` and later `Skill {"skill":"frontend-redesign"}`, but no `frontend-design` invocation.

summary: Fail: the functional editor workspace behavior is present, but `src/index.css` still hard-codes the Script and Preview panel surface colors instead of using design tokens, so the feature does not fully satisfy its own expected styling behavior.
